behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure appears at a module handoff where git-derived branch facts (ahead/age) are combined with PR-state lookup; mismatched field semantics can misclassify staleness even if each module works alone. Rejected System: behavior text only requires identifying conditions ("commits not in main", "older than threshold", "no open PR") and does not require real HTTP/subprocess to reproduce classification failure. Integration-brake check: (a) real OS/network/subprocess not required, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector + git-state adapter + PR-state adapter in-process using fixture branch metadata and PR records; assert stale=true only when all three predicates hold, including boundary cases around threshold age.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: bug risk is at bootstrap wiring (session-start hook consumes detector output and formatter context), where stale state may not propagate to emitted warning payload. Rejected System: behavior text is about "session-start setup emits ... warning" and can be exercised in-process without real OS/network/subprocess. Integration-brake check: (a) no real infra required, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize session bootstrap with detector result stubbed as stale and real warning formatter/reporting modules wired together; assert one warning is emitted containing branch name, age, and next action (e.g., open PR).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: No boundary escalation proof exists: behavior explicitly targets "a local status-classifier function" and deterministic label mapping logic. Rejected Integration: text confines scope to one local function, not module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Pure function table test over representative computed states, asserting exact label output and deterministic repeatability across repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: command rendering depends on handoff between repo-state collector, PR-state collector, classifier, and renderer. Integration->System proof: behavior explicitly says "against live repo and PR state"; real git/subprocess and real API/network behavior are required to catch failures hidden by mocks (e.g., auth, pagination/state freshness, CLI/environment differences). Rejected Agentic: no correctness dependence on model output quality/non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo with actual branch divergence and real PR state source; verify rendered output switches between `clean` and `STALE` as live state changes.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure occurs when PR lifecycle state from PR module is interpreted by stale classifier module; state mapping errors at this interface create false positives. Rejected System: behavior text only requires correct treatment of "open or merged PRs" and does not require live network/subprocess to expose the classification bug. Integration-brake check: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-step agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with PR-state provider and branch-state provider in-process; feed cases for open PR and merged PR and assert label is never `stale-no-pr`.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: detector correctness depends on interaction between commit-ancestry computation and stale classifier inputs. Integration->System proof: rebase/squash semantics and rewritten commit hashes are git graph behaviors that are reliably exposed only with a real repository and git subprocess operations; mocks commonly hide ancestry edge cases. Rejected Agentic: no LLM/ML output-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, create stale branch, then perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite hashes; run detection end-to-end and assert branch is no longer reported as unPRed stale when work is represented in main/merged history.
